attacking_style: Pure sprinter who capitalizes on tactical sprint finishes when the peloton controls or mistimes breakaway catches. Executes final sprint in closing kilometers.
team_tactics: Team positioning allows control of intermediate sprint points to accumulate classification points. Relies on peloton dynamics rather than dedicated lead-out train based on available data.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where breakaways are caught late. Thrives when peloton misjudges timing, creating tactical sprint scenarios rather than organized lead-out finishes.
vulnerability: Insufficient data on mountain stages, organized sprint trains, or positioning in chaotic finales. Single race sample limits tactical vulnerability assessment.
key_patterns: Multiple stage wins within single Grand Tour showing sustained sprint form (3 wins)
Targets intermediate sprints to build points classification lead systematically
Exploits tactical uncertainty when peloton catches breakaway in final 2km
form_trajectory: positive: demonstrated dominant and consistent sprint form with three stage victories while simultaneously controlling points classification
